verses.latin_vulgate: Et oravit Jonas ad Dominum Deum suum de ventre piscis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתְפַּלֵּ֣ל יוֹנָ֔ה אֶל־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהָ֑יו מִמְּעֵ֖י הַדָּגָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonah prayed to the Lord, his God, out of the belly of the fish.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jona rukoili Herraa Jumalaansa kalan vatsassa,
verses.king_james: Then Jonah prayed unto the Lord his God out of the fish’s belly,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dixit :   Clamavi de tribulatione mea ad Dominum,  et exaudivit me ;  de ventre inferi clamavi,  et exaudisti vocem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר קָ֠רָ֠אתִי מִצָּ֥רָה לִ֛י אֶל־יְהֹוָ֖ה וַֽיַּעֲנֵ֑נִי מִבֶּ֧טֶן שְׁא֛וֹל שִׁוַּ֖עְתִּי שָׁמַ֥עְתָּ קוֹלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: I cried out of my affliction to the Lord, and he heard me: I cried out of the belly of hell, and thou hast heard my voice.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi: minä huusin Herraa minun ahdistuksessani, ja hän vastasi minua; minä paruin helvetin vatsasta, ja sinä kuulit minun ääneni.
verses.king_james: And said, I cried by reason of mine affliction unto the Lord, and he heard me; out of the belly of hell cried I, and thou heardest my voice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et projecisti me in profundum in corde maris,  et flumen circumdedit me :  omnes gurgites tui, et fluctus tui super me transierunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּשְׁלִיכֵ֤נִי מְצוּלָה֙ בִּלְבַ֣ב יַמִּ֔ים וְנָהָ֖ר יְסֹבְבֵ֑נִי כׇּל־מִשְׁבָּרֶ֥יךָ וְגַלֶּ֖יךָ עָלַ֥י עָבָֽרוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thou hast cast me forth into the deep, in the heart of the sea, and a flood hast compassed me: all thy billows, and thy waves have passed over me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä heitit minun syvyyteen keskelle merta, niin että aallot minua pyörsivät ympäriinsä; kaikki sinun lainees ja aaltos kävivät minun päällitseni.
verses.king_james: For thou hadst cast me into the deep, in the midst of the seas; and the floods compassed me about: all thy billows and thy waves passed over me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego dixi :  Abjectus sum a conspectu oculorum tuorum ;  verumtamen rursus videbo templum sanctum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֣י אָמַ֔רְתִּי נִגְרַ֖שְׁתִּי מִנֶּ֣גֶד עֵינֶ֑יךָ אַ֚ךְ אוֹסִ֣יף לְהַבִּ֔יט אֶל־הֵיכַ֖ל קׇדְשֶֽׁךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I said: I am cast away out of the sight of thy eyes: but yet I shall see the holy temple again.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä ajattelin minuni hyljätyksi sinun silmäis edestä; kuitenkin saan minä vielä nähdä sinun pyhän templis.
verses.king_james: Then I said, I am cast out of thy sight; yet I will look again toward thy holy temple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circumdederunt me aquæ usque ad animam :  abyssus vallavit me,  pelagus operuit caput meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲפָפ֤וּנִי מַ֙יִם֙ עַד־נֶ֔פֶשׁ תְּה֖וֹם יְסֹבְבֵ֑נִי ס֖וּף חָב֥וּשׁ לְרֹאשִֽׁי׃
verses.douay_rheims: The waters compassed me about even to the soul: the deep hath closed me round about, the sea hath covered my head.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vedet piirsivät minua ympäri sieluuni asti; syvyydet piirittivät minun; kaislisto peitti minun pääni.
verses.king_james: The waters compassed me about, even to the soul: the depth closed me round about, the weeds were wrapped about my head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad extrema montium descendi ;  terræ vectes concluserunt me in æternum :  et sublevabis de corruptione vitam meam, Domine Deus meus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְקִצְבֵ֤י הָרִים֙ יָרַ֔דְתִּי הָאָ֛רֶץ בְּרִחֶ֥יהָ בַעֲדִ֖י לְעוֹלָ֑ם וַתַּ֧עַל מִשַּׁ֛חַת חַיַּ֖י יְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: I went down to the lowest parts of the mountains: the bars of the earth have shut me up for ever: and thou wilt bring up my life from corruption, O Lord, my God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä vajosin vuorten perustukseen asti; maa salpasi minun teljillänsä ijankaikkisesti; mutta sinä otit minun elämäni ulos kadotuksesta, Herra minun Jumalani.
verses.king_james: I went down to the bottoms of the mountains; the earth with her bars was about me for ever: yet hast thou brought up my life from corruption, O Lord my God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum angustiaretur in me anima mea,  Domini recordatus sum :  ut veniat ad te oratio mea,  ad templum sanctum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּהִתְעַטֵּ֤ף עָלַי֙ נַפְשִׁ֔י אֶת־יְהֹוָ֖ה זָכָ֑רְתִּי וַתָּב֤וֹא אֵלֶ֙יךָ֙ תְּפִלָּתִ֔י אֶל־הֵיכַ֖ל קׇדְשֶֽׁךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: When my soul was in distress within me, I remembered the Lord: that my prayer may come to thee, unto the holy temple.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin minun sieluni epäili minussa, niin minä muistin Herraa; ja minun rukoukseni tuli sinun tykös, sinun pyhään templiis.
verses.king_james: When my soul fainted within me I remembered the Lord: and my prayer came in unto thee, into thine holy temple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui custodiunt vanitates frustra,  misericordiam suam derelinquunt.
verses.douay_rheims: They that in vain observe vanities, forsake their own mercy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jotka luottavat turhaan valheeseen, ne hylkäävät laupiuden itse kohtaansa.
verses.king_james: They that observe lying vanities forsake their own mercy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem in voce laudis immolabo tibi :  quæcumque vovi, reddam pro salute Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֗י בְּק֤וֹל תּוֹדָה֙ אֶזְבְּחָה־לָּ֔ךְ אֲשֶׁ֥ר נָדַ֖רְתִּי אֲשַׁלֵּ֑מָה יְשׁוּעָ֖תָה לַיהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: But I with the voice of praise will sacrifice to thee: I will pay whatsoever I have vowed for my salvation to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta minä tahdon uhrata sinulle kiitoksella, minä tahdon maksaa lupaukseni; Herran tykönä on apu.
verses.king_james: But I will sacrifice unto thee with the voice of thanksgiving; I will pay that that I have vowed. Salvation is of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit Dominus pisci, et evomuit Jonam in aridam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְהֹוָ֖ה לַדָּ֑ג וַיָּקֵ֥א אֶת־יוֹנָ֖ה אֶל־הַיַּבָּשָֽׁה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the Lord spoke to the fish: and it vomited out Jonah upon the dry land.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herra sanoi kalalle, että hän oksensi Jonan maan päälle.
verses.king_james: And the Lord spake unto the fish, and it vomited out Jonah upon the dry land.
